explanation.rule: Suffixes like -ful, -less, and -ly change nouns or adjectives into new words that describe qualities or manner.

groups.note: Added to nouns to form adjectives meaning 'having' (-ful) or 'without' (-less)

examples.definition: helpful or having a practical purpose
not helpful; having no practical purpose

groups.note: Added to adjectives to form adverbs describing how something is done

examples.definition: to a high degree; very much

groups.note: Added to verbs to form adjectives meaning 'can be done'

wordForms.example: The country needs a powerful leader in these difficult times.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the ability to control or influence people or things

wordForms.definition: helpful or having a practical purpose
wordForms.example: This guidebook is really useful for first-time visitors.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to do something with a tool or method

wordForms.definition: a small number or as much as a hand can hold
wordForms.example: Only a handful of students passed the advanced exam.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the end part of the arm below the wrist

wordForms.definition: not helpful; having no practical value
wordForms.example: Without electricity, the device is completely useless.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to do something with a tool or method

wordForms.example: She leads a very active lifestyle and goes to the gym daily.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to do something or perform a role

wordForms.definition: in an energetic or engaged way
wordForms.example: He is actively looking for a new job.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to do something or perform a role

wordForms.definition: for a short time or in few words
wordForms.example: She briefly mentioned her new project at the meeting.
wordForms.rootDefinition: lasting a short time or using few words

wordForms.definition: very much; to a great extent
wordForms.example: He was deeply moved by the documentary.
wordForms.rootDefinition: extending far down from the surface

wordForms.definition: scarcely or almost not at all
wordForms.example: I could hardly hear the speaker from the back row.
wordForms.rootDefinition: with a lot of effort or force

wordForms.definition: to a great degree; very much
wordForms.example: She is highly regarded by her colleagues.
wordForms.rootDefinition: at a great distance above the ground

wordForms.example: The price seemed reasonable for the quality of the meal.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a cause or explanation for something

wordForms.definition: worth a lot of money or very useful
wordForms.example: Her experience in the field is extremely valuable.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the importance or worth of something

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The new manager is ___ and has changed everything for the better.
The instructions were so complicated they were completely ___.
Could you ___ describe what happened at the meeting?
He was ___ disappointed when he heard the news.
That was a very ___ answer – you clearly understand the topic.
The old painting turned out to be extremely ___.

transformExercises.sentence: The earthquake was a very ___ reminder of nature's force. (POWER)
She found the dictionary app incredibly ___ for her studies. (USE)
The speaker was so nervous she could ___ speak. (HARD)
He is ___ respected in the scientific community. (HIGH)
They were both ___ affected by the loss of their home. (DEEP)
Your feedback has been ___ in shaping our new approach. (VALUE)
